Notice Of Motion For Deposition Of Undistributed Client Files; Discharging The State Bar Of California
Off Calendar Apr-05-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MANUEL JIMENEZ, DEPUTY TRIAL COUNSEL, 415.538.2288, FOR STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA.
DAVID K. REPLOGLE, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER.

RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. THE COURT GRANTS PLAINTIFF STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Deposition Of Undistributed Client Files; Discharging The State Bar Of California. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Notice Of Motion For Deposition Of Undistributed Client Files; Discharging The State Bar Of California
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Continued To Apr-05-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA's Motion For Deposition Of Undistributed Client Files; Discharging The State Bar Of California is Continued to April 5, 2013 on the Court's own motion so David Replogle can be present by telephone. A hearing will be required on April 5, 2013 at 9:30 a.m. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2012 Continued To Feb-06-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Application For Order Authorizing State Bar Of California
Off Calendar Jul-26-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Manuel Jimenez, Esq., of the State Bar of California, Tel. 415.538.2288, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party;
No other appearance.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA Application For Order Authorizing State Bar Of California To Release Files To Federal Magistrate Nathaniel M. Cousins pursuant to a Federal Court Reference Order, Or In The Alternative, Request For Guidance From The Court - The State Bar possesses documents obtained after it took over the practice of an erstwhile lawyer, Replogle (Replogle Documents). The Replogle Documents may include things subject to attorney-client privilege and may relate to a pending suit in the North District of California, Roe v. White (03-04035) . The Replogle Documents likely also include many items not related to that case. The State Bar seeks instruction from this Court on how to respond to what is in effect a request from the federal court to provide the Replogle Documents for use in that case. Magistrate Judge Cousins has been directed to review any produced documents in a reference order of May 18, 2012 (Reference Order). The Reference Order in turn cites a stipulated protective order of November 15, 2011 which provides for the confidential treatment of certain papers and things (Protective Order). The Reference Order directs Judge Cousins to examine documents "defined in paragraph 2" of the Protective Order, but Paragraph II (there is not one literally denominated "2") does not contain such a definition. However, Paragraph I plainly contemplates a judicial review of the Replogle Documents, and I understand the Order is further limited to those documents which pertain to Roe v. White. Protective Order at 1 line 6 (re: case "against Defendant"). Thus the only documents Judge Cousins could possibly release to anyone would Replogle Documents related to Roe v. White. I further understand it that if Judge Cousin found through an in camera inspection that Roe v White documents were protected by an attorney-client privilege, those things would not be disclosed (except to the clients and their counsel) but instead would be noted in a privilege log. Thereafter, of course, federal litigation may take place in order to resolve an asserted claim of attorney client privilege. Under these circumstances, and assuming any document not relating to Roe v. White will be returned to the State Bar, the State Bar is authorized to provide the Replogle Documents to Judge Cousins. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
